title: Graphs of $y = |f(x)| where f(x)$ is Linear - Easy

questions.question: State the coordinates of the vertex of the graph y=∣x+6∣.
questions.explanation:
The vertex of y=∣x-h∣ is at (h,0). Here x+6=0 gives x=-6.

**Common mistake:** Misidentifying the sign of the x-coordinate, picking 6 instead of -6.

questions.question: Find the y-intercept of the graph representing y=∣3x-12∣.
questions.explanation:
Set x=0: y=∣3(0)-12∣=∣-12∣=12.

**Common mistake:** Identifying the x-intercept (4) as the y-intercept or failing to take the modulus and giving -12.

questions.question: State the equation of the axis of symmetry for the graph y=∣x-4∣+2.
questions.explanation:
The symmetry axis passes through the vertex. The vertex is at x=4, so the axis is x=4.

**Common mistake:** Using the vertical shift constant (2) or negating the horizontal shift incorrectly.

questions.question: For the basic function f(x)=∣x∣, find the image of x=-7.
questions.explanation:
By the definition of absolute value, ∣-7∣=7.

**Common mistake:** Failing to apply the modulus property and leaving the value as -7.

questions.question: Identify the coordinates of the minimum point on the graph y=∣2x-10∣+3.
questions.explanation:
The modulus is minimized at x=5 (2x-10=0). At x=5,y=3.

**Common mistake:** Neglecting the vertical shift (3) or failing to solve for the correct x-coordinate within the modulus.
